title: Build the factory kill switch (spec + minimal working implementation) + the voice read-only law
source: Mark Kashef distillation follow-up #6+#7 + David go 2026-07-06
prompt: Build a factory-wide kill switch and a voice read-only law, across dark-factory + the two pulse apps (omi-fetch, app-registry). Flag-file convention: engine/store/HALT. engine/halt.py (halt/resume/status) + bin/factory-halt + bin/factory-resume wrappers. orchestrator.py checks HALT before every claim/dispatch (skip + log); warm_pool.py workers don't get new sends while halted. Graceful only -- nothing killed mid-task. omi-fetch/pulse.py and app-registry/probe.py check the HALT path at start, log 'halted, skipping', exit 0 -- additive, never crash on a missing/unreadable path. status.py shows a big HALTED banner (+ --json); app-registry's view shows a halted banner if HALT exists at render. docs/kill-switch-spec.md documents the convention, scope, non-goals (no process kills), cross-machine note (per-machine flag for now). One short voice-law section appended to docs/comms-flow.md: voice-triggered surfaces are constitutionally read-only. Local commits in all three repos, do NOT push.
verify: halt.py halt -> run omi-fetch pulse manually -> its log shows 'halted, skipping' -> status.py shows HALTED banner -> halt.py resume -> pulse ticks normally again, with the transcript captured in this ticket's result.

commits.dark-factory: 304e64b feat(engine): factory kill switch (HALT flag-file) + voice read-only law (local, not pushed)
commits.omi-fetch: bb62556 feat(pulse): respect dark-factory's factory-wide HALT kill switch (local, not pushed)
commits.app-registry: ee52e55 feat(probe+view): respect dark-factory's factory-wide HALT kill switch (local, not pushed)

verify_transcript: store/pulse.log tail -> 2026-07-06T07:41:12.788157+00:00 fetched=0 new=0 errors=halted, skipping
store/probe.log tail -> 2026-07-06T07:41:18.287719+00:00 total=0 running=0 dark=0 unknown=0 unprobed=0 errors=halted, skipping
$ python3 engine/status.py   -> prints big '##  FACTORY HALTED  ##' banner with since/by/reason/flag
{'bootstrapped': False, 'fetched': 26, 'new': 0, ...}  -- ticked normally, pulse.log -> fetched=26 new=0 errors=-
Also verified: bin/factory-halt + bin/factory-resume wrappers work identically to halt.py directly;
app-registry lib/view.render() shows the red 'FACTORY HALTED' banner when called directly while HALT is set
(isolated call, not written to disk) -- confirms the banner fires at render time independent of probe.py's own early-exit.
result.notes: Ticket-first per instruction: results appended directly into this ticket JSON, file left in running/ (not moved to done/ -- that's the orchestrator's reap job). orchestrator.py's HALT check (claim/dispatch gate) and warm_pool.py's free_worker() gate were implemented and code-reviewed but not exercised end-to-end (would require booting a tmux worker pool, out of scope for the 10-second verify test named in the ticket) -- the two pulses' checks are the ones proven live per the verify bar above.
